lines: Ally Dark Presence, Saint George will support the Hunters
(Ally Dark Presence)
C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
217
Once upon a 
time…
There was a brave and devoted fighter who 
made the fight against monsters his reason 
for living. Saint George is perhaps the most 
important and powerful member of the 
Order, but his zeal in punishing the Broken 
Tales can be a threat to the Hunters too... 
Perhaps the real enemy does not hide in the 
shadows, but walks beside them...
The story that led us here…
Saint George the Dragon Slayer is a special Scenario 
that can be used during any Campaign or for a short game. 
In fact, once Saint George has been included as a support to 
the Hunters, he will have several methods of foiling plans, 
whether laid by friends or foes. Saint George will fight 
alongside the Hunters, but his stern gaze and inquisitorial 
spirit must be kept at bay so that the Saint does not unleash 
the fury of the Church against its own agents.
How to use Saint George
Saint George will join the group of Hunters as soon as they 
have solved a few Scenarios and earned a bit of fame. As an 
Ally Dark Presence, Saint George will support the Hunters 
on their travels. However, it will soon become clear that 
his zeal for everything Broken Tales-related will make him 
difficult to manage. 
Saint George possesses a Gift called The Judgment of 
the Saint. When enough Suspicion Tokens have been 
accumulated, the Saint will become an Opponent Dark 
Presence and will try to arrest the Hunters as he sees them 
as representing the darkness that the Church fights.
Saint George  
(Ally Dark Presence)
Once upon a time there was a cheerful child named George 
who often wandered away from home to run and play in the 
woods. One day, George fell into the river and in the blink 
of an eye, he was drowning. On the verge of death, the child 
felt an incredible force pushing him to the surface and soon 
found himself on the bank of the river, soaked to the bones 
but still alive. His savior was a strange, lizard-like creature 
with incredibly human eyes. A Dragon! thought the child, 
throwing himself on the creature’s neck, and a deep friendship 
was born between the two. In the following years, George 
took every opportunity to meet his secret friend and learn 
from him the wonders of magic, and what is hidden from 
the eyes of ordinary people. Then, a plague struck the village 
where George lived, killing his parents. The lad’s appeals for 
the Dragon’s help fell on deaf ears, despite the creature being

lines: and becomes an Opponent Dark Presence.
C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
218
capable of healing any disease. The Dragon, out of fear of being discovered 
and killed, refused to heal the people of the village. Twenty years passed 
and George took his vows, becoming a Holy fighter in the service of the 
Papacy. He led more than one battle against the forces of evil. When the 
Saint returned to his old village, he walked into the forest in search of 
his old friend. The Dragon was happy to see George again after such 
a long time and came out... only to realize that he had come before his 
executioner.
OL Medium 5 – (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a Holy knight in the service of the Church. I am skilled 
at hunting and defeating any monster. Nothing escapes my inquisitive gaze.
Agenda: Find every creature linked to the Broken Tales and exterminate 
them so that they can do no harm / Truly understand the nature of the Broken 
Tales.
Gift - The Holy Dragon Slayer:  Saint George starts the Scenario with 1 Fervor 
Token. When facing an enemy that is visibly monstrous, he can spend 1 Fervor Token 
to allow an allied Hunter to inflict 1 additional Wound. When such an enemy unveils one 
of their Gifts, Saint George can spend 1 Fervor Token to grant 2 Additional Successes on 
a Defense Check to any allied Hunter. Whenever he suffers 1 Wound, Saint George 
receives 1 Fervor Token.
Gift - The Judgment of the Saint:  Saint George keeps everyone under 
observation, even the Hunters he accompanies. During a Scene, if a Hunter 
activates their Dark Ego or uses any Gift that allows them to employ 
magic or terrorize ordinary people in the presence of Saint George, add 
1 Suspicion Token on this Gift. During a Scene, Saint George can keep a 
Hunter under observation, making them uncomfortable. When 
he does, the Storyteller may spend 1 Suspicion Token to 
impose 1 Penalty to a Hunter Check. Suspicion Tokens 
carry over from one Scenario to another. When there 
are Suspicion Tokens equal to twice the number of 
Hunters, Saint George engages in a Witch Hunt 
and becomes an Opponent Dark Presence.

lines: as a Dark Presence (page 49). Saint George will
(Opponent Dark Presence)
C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
219
How to manage Saint George
Once introduced in the Campaign or in the single Scenario, 
Saint George will be a recurring presence to whom the 
Hunters must be accountable. As the highest representative 
of the Papacy, the Saint has full authority over all members 
of the Order.
For the Hunters, it will be clear that Saint George could be 
a good ally, but that under his stern gaze not everything is 
permitted.
 ◆ The Storyteller can add Saint George to the party 
throughout the Scenario. Even if he has the authority 
to make all the decisions, it wouldn’t be fun to play the 
Session with an NPC leading the Hunters by a leash. The 
Saint will therefore remain in the background, curious 
to observe the Hunters and their work as members of 
the Order. The Storyteller must immediately make it 
clear that the Saint is at their disposal with his Gifts, 
but that The Judgment of the Saint can quickly turn 
him into an enemy.
 ◆ The Storyteller can add Saint George to the party, 
but the Saint will act independently and appear 
only in some Scenes. In this way, the Saint will apply 
more pressure on the Hunters, judging behaviors that 
he considers unbecoming for a member of the Order. 
He will always give support with his Gifts, but the 
Storyteller will be able to use him more as a nuisance 
than anything else.
 ◆ The Storyteller will only let Saint George step in 
when it is clear that a monstrous creature is featured 
in the Scenario. For example, in Red-Hood Iskra he 
could come after the appearance of Greskar (page 
125) or due to the presence of The Ancient Enemy 
as a Dark Presence (page 49). Saint George will 
arrive driven by a premonition that there is a monster 
to hunt down and smite. The Saint will be open and 
supportive towards the Hunters, at least until he has 
accumulated the first Suspicion Token. This is a good 
way to introduce Saint George to a Campaign for the 
first time.
 ◆ Once the Suspicion Tokens accrued are twice the 
number of Hunters, Saint George will launch a 
Witch Hunt, and will become an Opponent Dark 
Presence with the corresponding profile. From that 
moment on, like any Opponent, the Storyteller will be 
able to use him to impede the Hunters and to create a 
special Scenario leading to the final confrontation with 
Saint George.
Saint George 
(Opponent Dark Presence)
The Holy Fighter who eradicates darkness in all its forms. 
There are skilled Paladins in his service, ready to carry out 
his every command.
OL Medium 5 – (4 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a devout knight in the service of 
the Church. I am adept at hunting and capturing any 
manifestation of darkness. I know every Hunter in the Order 
and I know how to defeat them.
Agenda: Find the Hunters who have shown to be linked to 
the Broken Tales and lock them up in the Church’s prisons. 
Whoever opposes my sentence will perish, suffering the 
judgment of God himself.

lines: Dark Presence, Saint George acquires a number of Fervor
C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
220
Gift - Witch Hunt:  When he becomes an Opponent 
Dark Presence, Saint George acquires a number of Fervor 
Tokens equal to the number of Hunters. When facing a 
Hunter he’s been tracking down, he can spend 1 Fervor 
Token to inflict 1 additional Wound on top of the normal 
one, or just 1 Wound if the Hunter succeeds on their 
Defense Check. The only way to avoid this Wound is to 
have an Outcome with Increment and spend it to nullify 
the Gift’s effect. Saint George can spend 1 Fervor Token to 
transform any Position Check’s result for an action against 
him into a Failure so long as the Hunter performing it has 
at least one Descriptor related to magic or a monstrous 
characteristic.    Saint George regenerates Fervor Tokens 
equal to the number of Hunters at the beginning of each 
Scenario.
Gift - The Judgment of the Saint: Saint George keeps 
everyone under observation, even when hunting down 
Hunters. During a Scene, if a Hunter activates their Dark 
Ego to perform a good deed or uses any Gift that allows 
them to use magic or manipulate ordinary people to do 
a good deed in the presence of Saint George, he adds 1 
Judgment Token on this Gift. Judgment Tokens carry over 
from one Scenario to the next. When a number of Judgment 
Tokens equal to the number of Hunters are staked on this 
Gift, Saint George will make amends, declaring that he was 
wrong in his judgment, putting an end to the Witch Hunt.
Paladins of the Church
A group of faithful Paladins in the service of Saint George.
OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a Paladin in the service of the great Saint 
George himself and his word, for me, is law. Years of training 
have made me adept at fighting the Broken Tales.
Note: Each Paladin has a different Downside, indicated in 
the Paladin Gift.
Gift - Paladins: Each Paladin has a name and a dedicated 
Gift, and a Downside to add to the standard Descriptor. 
The Storyteller rolls 1 die to decide which Paladin follows 
Saint George in the Witch Hunt. Saint Geoge has a number 
of Paladins equal to that of the Hunters he hunts.

Note: Nasal ozena (also known as chronic atrophic
C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
223
OZena the Suffering and Desperate is a special Scenario 
divided into three parts, which when played consecutively 
completes the story of the unfortunate Dorothea. All 
Scenarios are built as a Scene Tree (see page 89) and 
are great for introductory Sessions or a mini-Campaign. 
A Storyteller who prefers an emerging storytelling style 
will still find Events and information useful for managing 
a freer playstyle for the Hunters.
Once upon a 
time…
Freiburg, West of the great Black Forest, 
Germany. The regent of the city, Nils Schulz, 
has invited the Order to investigate the 
activities of Margareta Westen, a renowned 
“witch” who is said to carry out strange 
experiments in a small village nearby. 
Although there is no apparent evidence of 
any heresy, the village of Gelb is shunned 
by all. Its inhabitants are deemed “strange” 
and it has, over the years, welcomed all 
manner of freaks and sick people looking 
for a safe place to live. Thus, the Hunters 
must go to Gelb and find out if Margareta 
is really the terrible witch that Nils fears.
The story that led us here…
Dorothea is a young American girl from the 
1900s who suffers from a very acute form of ozena. Under 
the care of the charlatan “Dr. ” Norman Henkle, she took 
part in a strange magical rite, and both were catapulted 
into the Black Forest of 1780. Here, under the guidance 
of Henkle, Dorothea took the name of OZena (a name 
that has not yet been made official for her disease). She’s 
hunting witches to procure ingredients and potions for 
her master, with the doctor promising to find a cure for 
the disease. Dorothea brought the Doctor other lost, 
ill people found along the way: Lowe, a scoundrel with 
Leontiasis; Dose, a thug suffering from leprosy who 
covers his skin with metal; and Hirnloser, a mental 
patient who believes he has no brain. 
After eliminating the witch Johanne in Schiltach (east of 
the Black Forest) from which she got her magic shoes, 
Dorothea arrived in Gelb with her companions. Here, 
pursuing the promise of her cure, she sent Lowe and 
Hirnloser to the village as spies to observe Margareta’s 
activities. The old witch is actually both learned and 
benevolent. She uses her magical knowledge to provide a 
home and support to many sick or deformed people who 
would otherwise have no place to live. 
Note: Nasal ozena (also known as chronic atrophic 
rhinitis) is a particular form of chronic rhinitis, 
characterized specifically by a marked and widespread 
atrophy of the nasal mucosa, so that the structure of 
the nose wastes away. The disease disfigures the face 
in a permanent way, affecting the appearance of those 
affected with devastating effects.

to the east, awaits a signal from Dorothea to spring into

The story at the arrival of the Hunters
Margareta Westen is a capable and experienced witch, 
who served at many noble courts as a fortune teller and 
counselor from a young age. Affected by a very serious form 
of deforming osteitis, which has ruined her legs and arms, 
Margareta has experienced the unreasoning prejudice of 
society herself. She was marginalized and abandoned by 
everyone. The witch has thus decided to found a small 
community, where all those like her would be welcome. In 
Gelb, Margareta cares for those unfortunate enough not to 
have a place in society, and most of the villagers would give 
their lives for her. 
Margareta is part of a coven of witches called the Compass 
Rose. Johanne, the witch of Schiltach (east of the Black 
Forest), was also part of the same coven. The death of her 
companion has put Margareta on alert, aware that it must 
have been someone who knew the secrets of the coven 
who killed Johanne. Dorothea has long since crept into 
Gelb, hiding at the edge of the small village. Hirnloser has 
instead openly joined the community, though everyone is 
wary of his mental illness. Lowe serves as eyes and arms for 
Dorothea around town, while Dose, hidden in the swamp 
to the east, awaits a signal from Dorothea to spring into 
action.
FIRST SCENE – Approaching 
the village of Gelb
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Before your eyes you see 
the great Black Forest in all its splendor, a refuge for animals 
and supernatural beings from the slow expansion of civilized 
life. A wide beaten path leads through the trees. Near a sign 
with directions to the village of Gelb, you see two motionless 
figures, as if they’re awaiting your arrival. One is a man, old 
and hunched over by who-knows-what disease, while the 
other is a child with a disfigured face. The two hold hands 
like father and son. The man nods to you and you understand 
that he is a villager sent to guide you.
Place: The road that winds its way from the west into the 
great Black Forest.
Time: It is early in the morning. A cart has brought the 
Hunters from Freiburg to the edge of the forest and the 
driver is now anxious to leave.
Initial Situation:  Henrich and Klaus approach the 
Hunters and introduce themselves as emissaries on 
Margareta’s behalf. The witch was formally notified by 
Nils that he was sending some of his agents to check on 
Gelb’s activities, but the regent took great care not to reveal 
the true nature of the Hunters. Henrich offers to lead the 
Hunters along the road to the village.
Information: Walking towards Gelb with Henrich and 
Klaus you can get the following clues and information.
 ◆ The village of Gelb is a safe place for anyone with a 
disease or a deformity that makes them an outcast in 
usual society. Margareta welcomes anyone without 
asking questions about their past and with the aid of her 
potions, she soothes the suffering of all the inhabitants. 
Although Henrich is a poorly educated farmer, he will

lines: ◆ Little Klaus is affected by Ozena and his nose is nothing
Klaus, the Ozena-
Agenda: To help Dorothea in her
C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
225
do nothing but praise Margareta’s goodness throughout 
the journey with sincere conviction.
 ◆ Little Klaus is affected by Ozena and his nose is nothing 
more than a clump of rotten meat. Despite the disease, 
he has the lively and curious eyes of a child, and he will 
ask the Hunters a thousand questions.
 ◆ The road the group is traveling on was created to 
facilitate commercial trips to Gelb. However, Henrich 
will regretfully inform the Hunters that once merchants 
arrive in the village, they are so frightened by the 
sight of the inhabitants, they hurriedly leave without 
conducting any business. 
 ◆ Halfway to the village, the Hunters will realize that 
someone is following them. It’s Lowe, who hides among 
the foliage and tries to figure out who the newcomers 
are.
End of Scene:  The Hunters arrive at the village of Gelb. 
Go to SECOND SCENE - The Village of Gelb.
Henrich the Father
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am one of the many people Margareta 
saved from the miserable life of an outcast. My son Klaus is 
everything to me, which is why I don’t want the village of 
Gelb to be suspected of witchcraft.
Klaus, the Ozena-
Afflicted Son

Lowe the Coward 
Henchman

who has been given a monstrous aspect

Agenda: To help Dorothea in her

SECOND SCENE – The Village 
of Gelb
Read aloud to the Hunters:  When you arrive at the 
entrance to the village, the spectacle that appears before you 
is incredible. People with all kinds of diseases and deformities 
walk in the streets, occupied performing a variety of tasks. In 
reality, what you see is nothing more than an ordinary village 
in Bavaria, inhabited solely by people who, in any other place, 
would be considered monsters.
Place: The small village of Gelb.
Time: It is morning, and all the inhabitants are busy with 
their work activities.
Information: Henrich will tell the Hunters that Margareta 
will soon arrive in the town square to dispense her medicines. 
While waiting for the witch, several things will happen.
 ◆ Hirnloser will appear, dancing and chanting of distant 
worlds that overlap each other. Adults look at him with 
pity, children walk around him amused. If the Hunters 
get close, he will try to interact with them. If the Hunters 
ignore him, he will be the one to approach. Whatever 
happens, when Margareta arrives in the square, 
Hirnloser will leave.
 ◆ When Margareta arrives all the inhabitants will stop 
working to run to her. The witch dispenses strange vials 
among happy and grateful faces. After the distribution 
of the medicines, Henrich will lead the Hunters to 
Margareta.
 ◆ In the crowd you can spot Lowe, who gazes at the potions 
with dreamy eyes.
 ◆ Dorothea is in the crowd too, her face covered with 
a heavy green hood, the smell of her illness clearly 
distinguishable. The girl will disappear using her shoes 
at the slightest hint of a problem.
 ◆ Margareta will speak to the Hunters, saying she fears for 
her life. A week ago, she received news of the murder 
of the witch Johanne who lived in Schiltach, in the 
eastern part of the Black Forest. Margareta is convinced 
that someone is trying to eliminate all 4 witches of the 
Black Forest, each of which holds part of a powerful 
artifact. She believes someone is gathering the pieces to 
reassemble the artifact, no matter the cost.
 ◆ The conversation will be interrupted by Lowe who 
screams desperately: Hirnloser has kidnapped Klaus 
and is taking him to the great swamp near the village! 
Margareta will ask the Hunters to save the child. In 
exchange, she will reveal to the Hunters what she knows 
of the artifact. In the woman’s eyes, the Hunters will see 
only a sincere concern for the fate of little Klaus.
Margareta the Good Witch
OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a capable witch who has devoted her life to 
caring for the unfortunate. My body is wracked with disease, 
but that doesn’t make me any less fearsome.
Agenda: Keep Gelb and everyone who lives there safe.
Gift - Witch:  Margareta can draw on her witch power 
to create a Descriptor to represent the effect of her magic. 
Her abilities are related to manipulating emotions, such as 
instilling fear or bestowing calm. The Descriptor thus created 
remains for the entire duration of a Scene. Alternatively, 
Margareta can move objects with the power of thought. These 
abilities can be used to hit someone from a distance, knock 
objects out of someone’s hand, and generally manipulate the 
environment.

Hirnloser the Scarecrow
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: Everyone thinks I’m crazy and maybe they’re 
right. I don’t feel any fear and I can see beneath the surface of 
those who pretend they are normal.
Agenda: I follow OZena because I can see past her pain. 
There is another world, the one where she comes from and 
where I want to go too.
End of Scene:  Margareta asks the Hunters to save little 
Klaus, offering to reveal all her secrets in exchange for the 
little one’s safety. If the Hunters set out to save Klaus go 
to SCENE THREE - In the Swamp . If, on the other hand, 
they decide to stay in the village or split up, for those who 
remain, go to the FOURTH SCENE - OZena’s Attack.
THIRD SCENE – In the Swamp
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You don’t have to go too 
far into the swamp before you hear little Klaus scream. You 
run to a small clearing where Hirnloser puts a sickle to the 
child’s throat. He is near an old, abandoned shed from which 
suddenly emerges a huge figure covered in grotesque armor 
made of debris. Hirnloser stares at you, his eyes pools of pure 
madness. “ And while we dance here in our little puppet show, 
the real performance unfolds in the village, ” the madman 
cries and smiles maliciously, as you realize that you have 
been lured into a trap.
Place: A clearing in the swamp twenty minutes’ walk from 
Gelb.
Time: It is late morning and the sun filters through the 
trees.
Information: Hirnloser and Dose want to engage the 
Hunters to give Dorothea time to kill Margareta. 
 ◆ Hirnloser is insane but he doesn’t really want to kill 
Klaus. It will soon become clear that he uses the child 
as a shield and lever to keep the Hunters busy.
 ◆ If Lowe is present, he will try to surprise the Hunters 
from behind but will flee at the slightest sign of danger.
Dose The Tin Man
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: Leprosy has destroyed my appearance, but 
my body is still that of the Mountain, the name by which 
I performed as a street artist in shows of strength and 
endurance. Under my heavy armor I fear nothing.
Agenda: I am in love with OZena, disfigured by the disease 
just like me. I am aware that she will never return my feelings 
and that is why the only thing I can do is serve her, even to 
my death.
Gift - The Tin Man: Dose is completely covered in thick 
armor made of metal and other salvaged materials, such as 
a large stove and the hull of a boat. The only unarmored spot 
is a small door in the stove that was once used to remove 
embers, which is located above his heart. It is not possible 
to hurt Dose unless one first finds a way to penetrate his 
armor or uses an attack that can clearly overcome his 
defenses.

End of Scene: After the fight, Klaus will tell the Hunters

FOURTH SCENE – OZena’s 
Attack
This Scene concludes the first part of the Scenario 
and will change based on whether the Hunters 
choose to leave someone in the village while they 
go to rescue little Klaus. Dorothea will attempt 
to kill Margareta in her house. If no Hunter 
is present, the witch will succumb to 
the blows of the young assassin. If 
any or all the Hunters are present, 
Dorothea will flee as soon as it 
becomes clear that she is at a 
distinct disadvantage.
Place: Margareta’s house. 
A large circular house built 
around a centuries-old oak.
Time: It is late morning and the village of Gelb has resumed 
its activity. Many inhabitants remain waiting tensely in the 
Town Square to discover the fate of little Klaus.
Information:
 ◆ During the fight, Dorothea’s hood will slide back and 
reveal her face. She is a young blonde girl, eyes of an 
intense blue and a suppurating chasm in place of her 
nose. 
 ◆ When Dorothea escapes and disappears, it will be clear 
to the Hunters that her slippers are magical.
 ◆ After the battle, if still alive, Margareta will reveal 
details on the Compass Rose. If she was killed by 
Dorothea, the Hunters will be able to obtain some of 
the information by examining the witch’s house.
OZena (Dorothea)
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a young girl who wants to return to 
her world. The disease that afflicts me has ruined my face 
and with it all the hopes of a normal life. I am willing to do 
anything to heal, which is why I became a witch killer.
Agenda: Kill every witch Henkle points out to me and 
retrieve all four pieces of the Compass Rose.
Gift - The Silver Slippers:  The wearer of this magical 
artifact can move through folds in space, disappearing and 
reappearing in a flash. Dorothea can move freely up to a 
maximum distance of thirty feet without anyone preventing 
her, but she must be able to see the point of arrival. Once 
per Scene, Dorothea can take a longer journey and reappear 
in any place she has seen at least once.

Epilogue
The Hunters will discover that OZena’s goal is the Compass 
Rose, a powerful artifact capable of summoning storms that 
are said to be able to throw people and objects into other 
worlds. The four Black Forest witches - Johanne (East), 
Margareta (West), Helga (South) and Karina (North) - split 
the artifact into four parts so that no one could abuse its 
power. Karina possessed the first piece of the Rose. Johanne 
had the second, as well as being famous for her magic 
slippers. For this reason, it is quite clear that OZena has 
already attacked and killed the first two witches. The next 
target will then be Helga, who lives in the village of Rot 
south of the Forest.
If Margareta is still alive, she will reveal some additional 
information. Helga is the cruelest and most powerful of the 
coven. Her magic is based on blood and, for this reason, 
the Hunters will have to be careful not to be injured in her 
presence. There is a potion that can break Helga’s blood 
curse, but Margareta doesn’t know its ingredients, apart 
from knowing it contains Burdock.
Note to the Storyteller:  OZena’s story continues with 
the Second Part, The Red Village. Keep track of who among 
Hirnloser, Dose and Lowe survived the Scenario. They will 
return in the third part, The Emerald Village.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are five Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
When the Hunters are in conversation with 
Margareta: Hirnloser will take the opportunity to 
kidnap Klaus and take him to the nearby swamp, creating a 
diversion to get the Hunters away from the village.
If the Hunters head to the swamps to save Klaus:  
Lowe will offer to “guide them” to try to waste more time 
and give Dorothea more leeway to kill Margareta.
If someone has wasted time interacting and 
getting to know Hirnloser in the village:  when the 
madman has Klaus with him, he will try to use the child 
as a lever to talk to his new friend again, forgetting about 
the plan.
If Dorothea is cornered:  she will flee thanks to her 
silver slippers. Dorothea cannot afford to risk being 
captured. This event occurs as soon as the situation turns 
in favor of the Hunters.
If Dorothea manages to kill Margareta:  she 
will recover the piece of the Compass Rose and then 
immediately escape thanks to her magical shoes.

Part Two – The 
Red Village
Safety Keywords: Rituals / Nudity / Blood / Body 
horror
Once upon a 
time…
Rot, South of the great Black Forest, Germany. 
After investigating Margareta Westen’s activities 
and discovering the secret of Gelb’s village, the 
Hunters are now aware of the threat from OZena, 
a young witch killer who is trying to rebuild a 
powerful magical artifact called the Compass Rose. 
As described by Margareta herself, whoever owns 
the Rose can invoke a storm so powerful, it can 
throw those who are swept up into other worlds and 
dimensions. OZena’s next target is Helga, a powerful 
witch who lives south of the great forest. Helga 
founded the cult of the Crimson Kiss, surrounded 
by faithful and devoted servants. The village of Rot, 
where Helga has made her home, is shunned even 
by state officials, who fear they won’t be able to 
leave that cursed place. But before going to Rot, the 
Hunters received a special invitation...
Principality of Transylvania, fortified church of 
Valea Viilor. Since the young OZena can move 
in space and time simply by clicking her magic 
shoes, the Hunters need an asset that allows them 
to anticipate the movements of the enemy. And 
the Order’s Treasury will surely have something 
suitable...
For this Scenario the Hunters will receive the support of 
the Order in the form of one of the many treasures kept in 
Valea Viilor.
The Mirror of a Thousand Wishes 
(Inspired by Snow White)
Gift - Mirror Mirror on the Wall: Those who use the 
Mirror of a Thousand Wishes can ask to see the near past 
or the imminent future of a subject they have met at least 
once. After spending 1 Soma, the Mirror will show a few 
moments of the target by displaying them on its surface. 
The observer will not know if what they see is the past or the 
future. The Storyteller secretly rolls a die: with 1-3 they will 
see the past, with 4-6 the future. The same subject cannot 
be observed more than once during a Scene. Alternatively, 
before attempting a specific task, the owner of the mirror 
can look into it and spend 1 Soma. Roll a die. If it’s a 4+ they 
will see a few moments of their imminent future. Thanks to 
this premonition they will receive 3 Additional Successes 
for the first action attempting to perform the task. 
Note to the Storyteller: The Order’s Treasure is an extra 
Gift that can be taken by one of the Hunters. The Broken 
Ones expansion book features complete rules for managing 
the Order’s Treasures and a long list of Treasures to choose 
from.

The story that led us here…
After the events in Gelb, Dorothea moved to Rot as the 
first step in eliminating Helga. The Red Witch, however, 
took the young woman by surprise. Thanks to the witches’ 
faithful servants, Helga captured the girl, holding her in 
her fortified villa. On the first night of the full moon, Helga 
intends to sacrifice Dorothea by performing a blasphemous 
rite which consists of drinking the victim’s blood to 
assimilate her memories. By doing so, Helga would learn 
of Henkle’s hiding place, where the other pieces of the 
Compass Rose are already located. 
Of course, the Hunters arrive only hours before the sun sets 
and a full moon rises, setting the stage for the ritual.
The story at the arrival of the Hunters…
Helga is a powerful witch who has never had any qualms 
about using dark magic. Through blood magic, she kept her 
appearance young (despite her eighty-eight years) and over 
time she built a true cult, whose foolish devotees would 
do anything for her. Through her blood Helga can control 
plants and people, moving them like puppets. Although the 
village of Rot has acquired a grim reputation, Helga knows 
it is only a matter of time before some obsessed clergyman 
decides to attack her to eradicate the evil she represents. 
The witch aims to obtain the Compass Rose to escape to a 
safe place, where she can rebuild her kingdom of fanatical 
servants.
FIRST SCENE – Towards the 
Village of Rot
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You have finally arrived in 
the deep south of the Black Forest. Along the way, there are 
numerous signs warning that the village of Rot is a cursed 
place. Dolls of wood and rag, crosses and mounds of salt, all 
bear witness to the pointless, superstitious spells of protection 
attempted by the inhabitants of the area. As you follow the 
small path to the village, the forest thickens more and more, 
while the branches of the trees lower as if to grab you.
Place: The small path that leads to Rot.
Time: It is late afternoon and the last rays of sunlight are 
struggling to pass between the branches of the trees.
Initial Situation: The branches seem to stretch lower and 
lower, until it becomes clear to the Hunters that something 
is wrong. Shortly after, the animated trees will try to catch 
the Hunters and imprison them.
Information: If a Hunter has some understanding of 
magic, they will quickly understand that what animates the 
trees is an arcane power. Observing the branches’ motions 
for a while, it will be possible to see a figure not far away 
bathing the trees in a liquid which apparently animates 
them.

 ◆ Future - Dorothea introduces herself to a beautiful 
young woman, dressed in an elegant red dress. The 
young assassin agrees to take a sip of the wine that is 
offered to her. Then, a trickle of blood runs down her 
lips and she falls to her knees. The woman in red smiles 
mockingly as a long knife falls from Dorothea’s hand.
Threat - The Animated Forest
OL Medium 5 
Descriptor: We grab and capture all intruders through the 
magic that animates our branches and roots.
Wolfgang the “druid”
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am a skilled and renowned pharmacist, and 
there is no herb or remedy that I am not familiar with.
Agenda: I am enslaved by Helga’s power. My body moves at 
her will and therefore I sprinkle drops of her blood to animate 
the forest to capture potential intruders.
End of Scene:  If captured, Wolfgang will reveal what 
he knows to the Hunters. In the greenhouse, other poor 
people like him work incessantly under Helga’s magical 
control. In the village, a strange figure called the Porcelain 
Princess ensures that no one can harm the witch. During 
the interrogation, Wolfgang will be seized with sudden 
spasms and drown in his own blood. If Wolfgang has been 
eliminated, the Hunters will simply have to choose which 
way to go.
If they go to the greenhouse, go to the SECOND SCENE - 
The Greenhouse.
If they go to the Village go to the THIRD SCENE - The 
Doll’s Court.
SECOND SCENE – The 
Greenhouse
Read aloud to the Hunters: Following the path you reach 
an old manor house which has been remade as a greenhouse. 
Many men and women dressed in white, with tired and long-
suffering expressions, work on small gardens. Strong smells of 
herbs and medicinal infusions fill the air.
Place: The greenhouse where the base for the Infusion of 
Purity is produced.
Time: The sun is setting. 
Information:
 ◆ Pharmacists are working on a strange potion. Those 
who are experts in herbs will recognize the plants as 
Burdock, Nettle and Hyssop. 
 ◆ If the Hunters approach the greenhouse, Manycorpses 
will drop from the ceiling to stop the intruders.
 ◆ The Infusion of Purity cannot be completed, as the 
Elderberry, which Dorothea took in her fight with 
Manycorpses, is missing.

Descriptor: I am an abomination created by Helga’s

Gift - Aberration: Manycorpses can act twice during an

End of Scene: After being freed, the greenhouse workers

blood magic. Something called the Infusion of Purity, a 
potion, can lift the curse. Helga is producing the Infusion

nobles after poisoning them with her magical blood. To 
complete the Infusion, however, Elderberry is required,

Go to the THIRD SCENE - The Dolls’ Court.
THIRD SCENE – The Dolls’ 
Court
Read aloud to the Hunters:  You come to a bleak, 
dilapidated village made up of small, ruined houses and 
brightly colored tents. Men and women in an evident state of 
distress lie sprawled along the streets. From their tired eyes, 
you understand that they are exhausted by the incessant work 
Helga magically forces them to do. Suddenly, figures wearing 
grotesque doll masks appear and head in your direction, 
drawing their weapons.
Place: The dilapidated village of Rot.
Time: The sun is setting, but time seems to have stopped 
in the small village.
Information:
 ◆ The Porcelain Dolls are the only individuals worth 
talking to. They will initially have their guard up, but if 
the Hunters are convincing, they will be brought to the 
Porcelain Princess.
 ◆ The village is just an old, dilapidated logging settlement. 
Not far away, it is possible to see a noble’s mansion 
where Helga now resides.
 ◆ No one can come before Helga without the approval of 
the Porcelain Princess, her favorite.

FOURTH SCENE – The Red 
Witch of the South
Read aloud to the Hunters:  Helga’s palace stands out 
from the whole village because it is the only well-maintained 
building. A heavy reinforced door and bars on the windows 
make you immediately understand that it will not be easy 
to enter. The soft sobbing of a girl catches your attention. It 
comes from the second floor, where there is a large balcony.
Place: The old mansion where Helga lives.
Time: It is now night and only the light of lanterns borne 
by the Hammerheads illuminate the mansion.
Information:
 ◆ Entering the main door means encountering

 ◆ Entering through the window will take the Hunters 
to the room where OZena is held. If the Hunters have 
an Infusion of Purity, they can free her from Helga’s 
control. From Dorothea’s green cape, thrown carelessly

Elderberry to complete a possible Infusion. Spending

 ◆ While the Hunters are busy in the mansion, Helga is 
taking a blood bath in a tub to prepare for the ritual. As

by blood magic. At the end of each of her actions and if hurt,

Part Three – The 
Emerald Village
Safety Keywords: Body horror
Once upon a 
time…
Smaragd, in the center of the great Black Forest. 
The Hunters are on the trail of Norman Henkle, 
a doctor and charlatan who apparently is the 
mastermind behind the young witch killer who 
goes by the name of OZena. The girl, whose real 
name is Dorothea, claims Henkle is actually 
a powerful wizard who fulfills wishes. His 
ultimate goal is to activate the Compass Rose 
to summon a storm and return to his own time. 
Given the young woman’s failure to retrieve the 
last two pieces, Dorothea fears that Henkle will 
activate the artifact anyway. Dorothea, however, 
remembers the first witch she killed saying that 
using the Compass Rose without all the pieces 
would be disastrous, opening a passage to 
another world, but at a very high price...
The story that led us here…
Norman Henkle has been settled in a small and remote 
village for a month, where he halted an epidemic thanks 
to the Penicillin in his doctor’s bag, brought back from the 
year 1900. 
Raised to the status of holy man, Norman began to believe 
himself a magician, giving free rein to his madness. In the 
center of Smaragd there is a small well whose true nature the 
inhabitants have always ignored. It is in fact a wishing well, 
which after centuries of inactivity, has awakened thanks to 
the desire of Henkle himself to become a magician. 
Now possessing new powers, Henkle is truly capable of 
making people’s wishes come true. However, his madness 
twists every wish and the results are always tainted. 
Determined to return to his time and become famous, 
Henkle lured young Dorothea into his service, promising 
to fulfill her one desire, to heal the disease that disfigures 
her face. 
Aware that he can’t really do it without risking some worse 
effect, Henkle is waiting for OZena’s return to retrieve the 
last two pieces of the Compass Rose so he can leave Smaragd, 
escaping in his hot-air balloon. In the meantime, Henkle 
has “experimented” with his power on the inhabitants of 
Smaragd, creating monsters and aberrations that now hope 
to earn a new wish to return to normal, unaware that they 
are locked in an endless cycle of warped wishes.

The story at the arrival of the Hunters…
Henkle fortified Smaragd against any possible intruder, 
garrisoned by the villagers now corrupted by their wishes, 
as well as the former companions of Dorothea who 
survived the first encounter with the Hunters. While the 
hot-air balloon is almost done, Henkle awaits Dorothea’s 
arrival to complete the Compass Rose and escape.
Note to the Storyteller: This part of the Scenario differs 
from the first two parts, as many enemies and encounters 
will depend on what the Hunters have done previously. For 
this reason, you’ll find only three Scenes leading up to the 
Hunters meeting Henkle. It is possible to drop one or more 
of the NPCs presented in each Scene to provide a challenge 
for the Hunters. At the end of each Scene, or during a Scene 
itself, if the Hunters face NPCs in a conflict, place 1 Time 
Token on Henkle’s Hot-Air Balloon Gift. Until the hot-air 
balloon is revealed, the Hunters will only know the name 
of the Tokens that are accumulated by the Storyteller, but 
not their purpose.
FIRST SCENE – Approaching 
the Village of Smaragd
Read aloud to the Hunters: Finally, you reach the center 
of the great Black Forest. A small path, now 
almost completely lost among    weeds, is the 
only point of reference to find your way. 
Dorothea precedes you by a few steps, 
visibly nervous. Suddenly you see the 
girl stop and she wavers for a moment, 
suddenly finding herself on the edge 
of a huge ravine. “This wasn’t here 
the last time I was here, ” exclaims 
the amazed girl. A moat over 
sixty feet deep and thirty wide surrounds a small village that 
you can glimpse through the foliage. The fact that there is 
no bridge suggests that it was created for the sole purpose of 
keeping intruders away.
Place: A huge circular pit that Henkle created in order to 
secure Smaragd
Time: It is early morning. The sun is high in the cloudless 
sky.
Initial Situation:  It will be clear to the Hunters that 
the moat must be crossed somehow. Dorothea can use 
her silver slippers if she still has them, but she won’t do so 
without the consent of the Hunters. The girl is convinced 
that the Order can help her and will not betray the trust of 
the Hunters, unless she sees a possibility of returning to the 
future (see Events).
Information:  Henkle’s plan is to hit the Hunters as 
they cross the pit, so he has deployed some underlings to 
ambush them. Choose one of the following:
 ◆ Hirnloser will have discovered a good position from 
which to target the Hunters at a distance with the help 
of 6 Villagers. 
 ◆ Lowe will go to meet the Hunters to challenge them

For this he has deployed some subordinates to set up an 
ambush. Choose one of the following:
 ◆ Hirnloser will plan an ambush with the help of 4

in the open field, bringing with him 3 Villagers and 3

End of Scene: The Hunters arrive at Henkle’s Castle, go to

FOURTH SCENE – The End of

The final battle with Henkle can go in many different ways. 
If he manages to activate the incomplete Compass Rose, 
a huge tornado will hit the whole Black Forest, affecting 
everyone and taking Henkle away to a place unknown to 
the Hunters.
Whatever happens, the fate of young Dorothea will be in

Lowe the Brave Fighter
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: I became a proud warrior and realized my 
heroic potential. I never back down and set an example for 
my teammates.
Agenda: Stop the invaders and protect the village of 
Smaragd, which has elected me as its hero.
Gift - Hero: Lowe fears nothing and is immune to all forms 
of fear and magical control. After he acts, his allies receive 
+1 to their OL, inspired by their hero’s courage and words.
Hirnloser the Genius
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: My mind has become infallible, and I can 
calculate and check any variable in seconds. I have a strategy 
for every situation.
Agenda: Solve any potential problems that plague my 
benefactor Norman Henkle.

Gift - Genius: When someone uses a Gift, Hirnloser can 
prevent it from activating by deploying a countering system 
or strategy. This Gift can be used up to 3 times during a 
Scene, but only 1 time per Exchange. 
Dose the Giant in Love
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)
Descriptor: My body has become pure metal and only the 
power of love can bring me back to flesh and blood. I fight 
and destroy anything that can threaten my sweet OZena.
Agenda: I am in love with OZena and I want her to loyally 
serve Henkle once more. I know that imprisonment in the 
hands of the enemy may have made her reluctant to trust us. 
I have to save her, whether she wants it or not.
Gift - Of Steel and Love: Dose is made of pure metal and 
cannot suffer any kind of physical Wound. The only way to 
get him to return to flesh and blood for a few moments is 
to make his heart beat with love. Seeing OZena in danger 
or well disposed towards him will change him back for 
one Exchange. Seeing OZena, Dose can’t help but declare 
his love as he fights, making his infatuation for the young 
assassin clear.
The Canine Tigers
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: We are the village dogs, turned into monsters 
by the desire of one of the children. Once the tiger’s ferocity 
takes over, we can no longer be controlled.
Gift - Fierce as a Tiger:  When a Canine Tiger suffers 
a Wound, it goes mad with rage and its Opposition Level 
rises to Hard 7. At the mercy of anger, the Tiger will attack 
the closest target without distinguishing between allies and 
enemies.
Norman  Henkle 
The Powerful Wizard Who 
Makes Wishes Come True
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (2 Wounds)
Descriptor: I am a powerful wizard and what I desire 
becomes reality. My skill as a barker always charms everyone 
to my side.
Agenda: I want to go back to my time alone and become a 
celebrity thanks to the power of magic.
Gift - Hot-Air Balloon: As soon as it is ready, Henkle will 
board and release his hot-air balloon into the air to activate 
the Compass Rose. As indicated by the Scenario, based on 
the actions of the Hunters this Gift will accumulate Time 
Tokens. When the third Time Token is placed, the hot-air 
balloon is ready to go.

The Villagers of Emerald
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: We are poor villagers changed by a wish we 
made. 
Agenda: We hate Henkle for what he did to us, but we serve 
him in the hope that we can make another wish.
Gift - Careful What you Wish For:  Each Villager has 
made a wish, but it has not had the desired effects. Roll 1 die 
to see what mutation has developed: 1. Wish for Wealth: 
The NPC spits out gold coins mixed with bile. With a single 
action, they can hit up to 3 targets at a distance of 15 feet; 
2. Wish for Beauty:  The NPC warps their body so as 
always to look beautiful in the eyes of the beholder. When 
there is more than one subject in view, their body changes 
constantly. Whoever witnesses the show receives 1 Penalty 
on their Checks out of disgust; 3. Wish for Health:  The 
NPC’s body regenerates as their hair and body hair grow 
out of proportion. The NPC recovers 1 Wound as soon as it 
loses 1, making them effectively immortal. The effect of the 
Wound suffered is applied, so the NPC can be defeated, but 
will regenerate on its turn to act; 4. Wish for Skill: The 
NPC raises their OL to Medium; 5. The NPC can no longer 
sleep because they are obsessed with the idea of working, 
and will constantly be chattering nonsense; 5. Wish to 
Grow Up: The NPC is a child with a body of a giant while 
their head has remained the same as before. They can take 
2 extra Wounds and inflict 1 additional Wound when they 
unleash their strength; 6. Wish for Love:  The NPC has 
merged with the one they love, literally obtaining the desire 
to “become one. ” As a double body the NPC can act twice.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are two Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
When Henkle realizes that Dorothea does not have 
the missing pieces of the Compass Rose with her:  
he will take off on the balloon as soon as it is ready, and 
then activate the power of the incomplete Rose.
If Dorothea realizes that Henkle is willing to 
abandon her to return to the future:  she will do 
anything to reach the man, begging him not to abandon 
her.

The Clues
The following is a series of Clues that the Storyteller must 
drop during the course of the Campaign.
 The Road of Memories
The Hunters find a diary written in a child’s handwriting. 
The little book has a powerful magical aura, from which 
emanates sadness and echoes of the past. The written pages 
clearly illustrate an incredible passion for fairytales and for 
all the characters who are part of them. The diary is full 
of drawings sketched with a child’s hand. They represent 
imaginary adventures starring the Hunters themselves, 
painted as heroes who save the world of fairytales. The last 
page of the diary is a drawing representing a large mansion 
at the end of a tree-lined avenue.
If the Hunters have already been to Borgo Maleanime they 
will recognize the avenue, but they will not remember 
having seen the mansion. If they have never been there, they 
will have the clear perception that the diary is a magical key 
to get to a hidden place. 
If they open the diary to the drawing of the mansion facing 
the tree-lined avenue in Borgo Maleanime, they will see the 
house.
 The Child Savior
A strange child wanders at night, crossing the path of the 
Hunters. His expression is sad and he never speaks. He can 
come and go anywhere he wants, appearing and disappearing 
in the blink of an eye. Sometimes he watches the Hunters, 
sometimes he helps them for no obvious reason. It will soon 
be clear to the Hunters that this figure is endowed with great 
power, but that he acts towards inscrutable ends.
The Child Savior is but a ghost, a reminder of who the One 
Who Plots in the Shadows once was. He does, however, 
still share some of the power that led to the creation of the 
world of Broken Tales. The Child Savior will approach the 
Hunters to study them, trying to understand if they can 
be the “heroes” who could bring peace to the heart of the 
One Who Plots in the Shadows. After their first meeting, 
the Child Savior could come to their aid using his ability 
to come and go from stories as he pleases. It is not possible 
to communicate with him in a normal way, but only by 
trying to interpret his actions. The use of certain Gifts, at 
the Storyteller’s discretion, may allow a Hunter to meet or 
speak with the Child Savior for a short time. 
Through his messages, the Child Savior can impart the 
following information:
 ◆ The Broken Tales are not really evil, but someone is 
warping their destiny.
 ◆ Someone pulls the strings of the Hunters’ fate.
 ◆ The Hunters find an original fairytale that tells the true 
story behind one of the Scenarios they have already 
faced.
 ◆ The location of the Diary (see The Road of Memories).
 Sherazade the Weaver of Tales
Sherazade threatens the Hunters thanks to her power to 
make nightmares real. As the agent of the One Who Plots 
in the Shadows, the Weaver of Tales wants to prevent the 
Hunters from uncovering the truth behind the Broken Tales.
Sherazade will act as described in the relevant Scenario on 
page 196. In the Campaign, the Weaver of Tales resides 
in Italy, in the Kingdom of Naples, as a guest of the noble

Bourbon family. The clues that her Shadows will leave must, 
therefore, lead the Hunters to the city of Naples. Some 
examples may be: the Bourbon coat of arms on a precious 
ring, a small lava stone from Vesuvius, or a key with a finely 
worked head depicting the royal palace of Caserta.
Once defeated, Sherazade will tell her story and how a 
strange man whose face she can’t remember gave her the 
power to take revenge for her sister’s death. Sherazade has 
tried several times to tell a story with this strange figure 
as the protagonist, but each time her power fizzled when 
doing so.
 ◆ All she has seen is an old mansion at the end of a tree-
lined avenue. 
 ◆ In another story, the mysterious figure did nothing 
but write, as if obsessed with the idea of modifying his 
stories. 
 ◆ In the past, Sherazade has seen a strange child appear 
in some stories, able to put an end to the magic of her 
nightmares.
 ◆ If the Storyteller thinks the time is right, Sherazade 
will describe having visited Borgo Maleanime the first 
time she received her powers and can lead the Hunters 
there.
 Saint George the Dragon Slayer
Saint George puts pressure on the Hunters by judging their 
integrity. As the unwitting pawn of the One Who Plots in the 
Shadows, the Dragon Slayer becomes a threat to Hunters who 
have gone too far, considering them villains.
Saint George will act as described in the relevant Scenario 
on page 216. In the Campaign, the Saint will have received 
his assignment from the Pope himself, but will clearly 
remember that at the side of the pontiff there was a strange 
adviser that no one had ever seen before. Investigating in 
Rome, it will be obvious that no one has any memory of 
this person except for his frequent speeches on the danger 
of the Hunters, a weapon in the hands of the Order that 
has always risked turning into a greater threat than what 
it fights.
After making amends, Saint George will vow to help the 
Hunters by setting out in search of this strange figure. Later, 
the Saint will be able to bring various information to the 
attention of the Hunters by acting as an intermediary for 
other Clues. For example, he could suggest that Niklaus 
von Krampus has already been to Borgo Maleanime.
 Memories of the Old Exorcist
Niklaus von Krampus has already been to Borgo Maleanime, 
where an encounter with a mysterious demon convinced him 
to become a Hunter. What Niklaus does not know is that his 
whole story is but the umpteenth creation of the One Who 
Plots in the Shadows, to remove Niklaus from the game before 
he can reveal too much. The old priest has always had the 
strong feeling of having left an unfinished exorcism behind in 
Borgo Maleanime.
Niklaus is known in Borgo Maleanime and the villagers will 
be well disposed towards him. They will show him kindness 
that they would not to any other foreigner. If Niklaus is 
not among the Hunters played, the Hunters will be able to 
learn what he did in Borgo Maleanime when reporting to 
the Pope in Rome, or from Saint George once he is on their 
side. The Hunters will discover that the old exorcist is on 
a mission for the Order and the Storyteller will be able to 
introduce a new Scenario with von Krampus as an NPC.

 The Assassin
The One Who Plots in the Shadows sends a Dark Presence to 
thwart the Hunters.
The Dark Presence will aim to impede the Hunters and, if 
defeated, will lead to a Clue taken from “The Confession. ”
 The Prophecy
A powerful group (such as the Zugarramurdi witches) or a 
Dark Presence (such as Ambrose, for example) will reveal to 
the Hunters a prophecy about a dark creator who gives birth 
to all the evil in Broken Europe.
The prophecy speaks of a human who became God and 
believes he holds the whole world in his hands. The prophecy 
will also reveal that the Child Savior, the protagonist of an 
ancient fairytale, has the power to stop the One Who Plots 
in the Shadows. This prophecy will reveal to the Hunters 
the name of their enemy and a place where the Child 
Savior was last seen. By choosing a location on the Map, 
the Storyteller will be able to drop the “Child Savior Clue” 
into the relevant Scenario.
 The Epilogue
Once the necessary Clues have been obtained, the Hunters 
will be able to see the path that leads to the mansion where 
the One Who Plots in the Shadows lives. The villain will 
unleash a group of Dark Presences against the Hunters, 
created ad hoc for the final confrontation. The Dark 
Presences must be equal to the number of Hunters and 
each must be linked to a Hunter’s personal story. These 
Dark Presences must have a background and an Agenda 
that leads them to hate the Hunters. 
Once the Dark Presences are defeated, the Hunters will face 
the One Who Plots in the Shadows. He is an anonymous 
man hunched over a large desk, scribbling incessantly on 
the pages of a book before tearing them out in anger. The 
One Who Plots in the Shadows will explain the whole true 
story of the Child Savior and how the world as the Hunters 
know it is nothing more than his creation.
The poor writer is now only a madman, prey to his own 
delirium of omnipotence. However, what will be clear to 
the Hunters is that his death will erase the history of Broken 
Europe. A story of which they are, in spite of themselves, 
the protagonists.
Killing the One Who Plots in the Shadows and bringing all 
the fairytales back to what they were will turn the Hunters 
back into being the villains, the losers, the ones everyone 
hates.
Leaving the One Who Plots in the Shadows alive will mean 
accepting a new world ruled by a mad God. But, it will be 
a world where Hunters have the free will to be whatever 
they want.
What will the Players choose?

The Map of Broken 
Europe
During the Campaign, the Hunters will move across 
the Map of Broken Europe. Some places will have a 
corresponding Scenario, while in others it will be possible 
to drop Dark Presences or encounters of various kinds. The 
Storyteller can modify at will the Scenarios and situations 
of each place according to the progress of the Campaign, 
but the following information is to be considered canon for 
how the general story of the Broken Tales was conceived.
 Black Forest
Here the Hunters can face a Scenario in three acts called 
OZena the Suffering and Desperate. Once the final scenario 
is completed, OZena could become a Hunter of the Order. 
OZena the Afflicted’s sheet is featured in the expansion 
book The Broken Ones.
 Borgholm
In this location, the Hunters will face the Scenario A 
Soldier’s Duty.
 Borgo Maleanime
Here, the Hunters can track the One Who Plots in the 
Shadows if they have collected enough Clues. It will also be 
the place of the final confrontation. Borgo Maleanime can 
only be accessed if led by someone who has already visited 
the town in the past.
 Devil’s Bridge
Europe is littered with bridges that manifest strange powers 
and that the local population always associate with the 
Devil. One Devil’s Bridge can lead Hunters to another such 
Bridge. Devil’s Bridges are spread across Europe, allowing 
the Hunters to cover great distances in no time. There is 
also a Devil’s Bridge in Borgo Maleanime, which could be 
an alternative way for the Hunters to get to the hideout of 
the One Who Plots in the Shadows.
 Durfort
In this place the Hunters will face the Scenario Red-Hood 
Iskra. Once the Scenario is resolved, Iskra could become 
a Hunter of the Order. Iskra the Y oung Wolf ’s sheet is 
featured in the expansion book The Broken Ones.
 Martana Island
In this place the Hunters will face the Scenario The House 
of Roses, featured in the expansion book The Broken Ones. 
Once this Scenario is completed, Beauty and her Beast 
could become Hunters of the Order. The sheet for Beauty 
and her Beast is featured in the expansion book The 
Broken Ones.
 Lohr
In this place the Hunters will face the Scenario 
Bloodwhite’s Heart, featured in the expansion book 
The Broken Ones. Once this Scenario is completed, 
Bloodwhite could become a Hunter of the Order. 
Bloodwhite’s sheet is featured in the expansion book 
The Broken Ones.

 London 
In this place the Hunters will face the Scenario I Don’t 
Believe, featured in the expansion book The Broken Ones.
 Malbork
This city is home to the Order of the Teutonic Knights, who 
have been in open conflict with the Church for years. The 
Teutonic Knights believe the Hunters have strayed from the 
straight path that should define a servant of God. 
Some possible situations related to the Knights:
 ◆ A Hunter of the Order has been taken prisoner (it 
could be Krampus, whom the Hunters may be hoping 
can lead them to Borgo Maleanime). 
 ◆ A Treasure linked to the Broken Tales has been found 
by the Knights and is jealously guarded in a stronghold. 
 ◆ The Knights are preparing an attack on Valea Viilor, 
with the intent of penetrating the fortified church and 
discovering its secrets.
 Naples 
In this city, the Hunters will be able to track down Sherazade 
and put an end to the threat she poses. Once the Scenario 
is resolved, Sherazade the Weaver of Tales could become 
a Hunter of the Order. Sherazade’s sheet is featured in the 
expansion book The Broken Ones.
 Nijmegen 
Here the Hunters can meet the last druids in all of Broken 
Europe. The druids are secretly in the service of the Dark 
Presence Ambrose the Sorcerer, known as Merlin. The 
druids are always ready to help the Hunters, but only 
because they secretly try to gather information about the 
Broken Tales to share with Merlin. 
Some possible situations related to Nijmegen:
 ◆ The druids ask for the Hunters’ help in driving the 
Dark Presence Oakheart from the forest. The spirit 
is in Nijmegen because she has fallen in love with a 
young druid.
 ◆ The druids ask the Hunters to capture a young novice 
who has betrayed their order and is on his way to 
Malbork to denounce the coven to the Teutonic 
Knights. If the Hunters help them, the druids will reveal 
the location of a Devil’s Bridge around Nijmegen.
 Paris 
In this location, the Hunters will face the Scenario The 
Smile in the Darkness. Once the Scenario is completed, 
Yvonne could become a Hunter of the Order. Yvonne of 
the Dark’s sheet is featured in the expansion book The 
Broken Ones.

 Zugarramurdi
In this small town, a powerful coven of witches has always 
been hiding. Led by the sorceress Gabrina Furetta, who 
fled from Italy after attracting too much attention from 
the Papacy, the witches are available to provide power and 
information. However, they always charge a price for their 
help.
 
Some possible situations related to Zugarramurdi:
 ◆ Saint George arrives in the small town with his Paladins 
to capture Gabrina.
 ◆ The Hunters arrive during a magical challenge between 
Baba Y aga and Gabrina. The two witches have a strong 
mutual dislike (also because Baba Y aga never misses an 
opportunity to mock her rival) and the consequences 
of the many spells cast threaten to shed a bad light on 
the Order.
Encounters
Encounters are Game Scenes that are outside a Scenario 
and come into play when the Hunters move from one place 
to another along the Map. The purpose of the Encounters 
is to give variety to the Campaign game, highlighting the 
stories and details of Broken Europe. 
When the Hunters travel, based on the distance between 
the place of departure and the place of arrival, the 
Storyteller prepares 1 to 3 Encounters. This proportion is 
purely subjective and only serves to give a perception of 
distance, but it isn’t intended to be realistic. Simply put, it 
will be clear to the Hunters that the longer a journey, the 
more situations will arise along the way.
An Encounter follows the normal rules for Scenes but 
ends when the Storyteller believes it has run its course. 
Hunters are entitled to a Scenario’s end Interlude after each 
Encounter. This is a good incentive for them, as Encounters 
are a good way to earn XP without having to face the risks 
of a full Scenario. 
When planning the Encounters, the Storyteller can be 
inspired by one of these 3 options:
Encounter with a Dark Presence
The Hunters stumble upon one of the Dark Presences 
featured on page 41. Roll a die or pick from the list:
Ambrose the Sorcerer, known as the Old Merlin 
1-2 The Hunters see an old, ruined tower in the mists. It is 
the current home of Ambrose. The old wizard will welcome 
them if they knock at his door. 3-4 A monstrous beast sows 
panic in the square of a small village. In reality, the beast 
was unleashed by Ambrose, curious to see the Hunters at 
work. 5-6 Ambrose approaches the Hunters in the guise of 
an old farmer named Kurt. The man is desperate because a 
fairy creature destroys his crops every night. Ambrose hopes 
the Hunters will eliminate the creature (which is actually 
innocent and just upset by the wizard’s presence) because its 
skin is useful for some arcane preparations.
Frog Face
1-2 Frog Face falls in love with one of the Hunters. 3-4 The 
Hunters run into a young girl on the run. Frog Face and his 
trusty Heinrich watch the encounter from a distance, waiting 
to be able to retrieve their prey. 5-6 Heinrich approaches 
the Hunters to request their services. His lord offers immense 
riches if they manage to break a curse that hangs on him...
1

Hua Mulan the Sword Demon  
1-2 The sound of a fight draws the attention of the Hunters. 
A very skilled warrior faces more than ten brigands alone. 
Will she need help? 3-4 Hua Mulan is on the trail of one of 
the Hunters to avenge an injustice they inflicted to a former 
Scenario NPC. 5-6 The Hunters find a half-dead deserter. 
After recovering, the young man will say his name is Patrick 
and will tell of having escaped the fury of an invincible warrior 
who is hunting him down to punish him for his desertion. 
Patrick will weep, saying that he was forced to enlist and that 
he is just trying to reunite with his wife and daughter.
The Periwinkle Peer
1-2 The Peer tracks down the Hunters to challenge the most 
skilled among them to a duel. 3-4 The Hunters meet the Peer 
at a village celebration, and amidst music and libations, the 
NPC will use his Gift of Divine Charm on one of the Hunters. 
5-6 The Hunters meet Isabella, a young woman with a sad 
look. She is a noble who has forsaken her family to follow the 
Periwinkle Peer, who has now abandoned her for another 
woman. If only someone would recover the necklace that 
Isabella gave him as a promise of marriage, the young woman 
would be free from all bonds and could return to her family.
The Ancient Enemy
1-2 A group of desperate farmers approaches the Hunters. A 
large beast comes out every night and eats the cattle, and no 
one is able to stop it. 3-4 The Ancient Enemy approaches the 
Hunters in the guise of a small squirrel. He wants to observe 
them in the next Scenario they will face, to understand if they 
can be true allies who could save him from Saint George and 
his Paladins. 5-6 A little girl named Sara asks the Hunters 
to deliver a flower necklace to the “big lizard” who saved 
her from drowning in the lake near the village. None of the 
adults believe her story, but she would love to repay her savior. 
Details in Sara’s description will give the Hunters the distinct 
impression that this encounter actually happened.
The Wild Swans Gang
1-2 The Hunters arrive in a village where the church has 
been set on fire by a group of fierce young people. Anton, the 
parish priest, will say that there were eleven criminals and 
that he heard them boast of their deep hatred for the papacy. 
Now Anton fears that the gang is headed for the church in a 
nearby village. 3-4 The Hunters stumble upon a dilapidated 
house that everyone says is the home of a witch. Traces of 
blood which lead into the nearby forest and disappear do not 
bode well. 5-6 Arriving in the city, the Hunters discover that 
another Hunter of the Order has arrived before them but has 
mysteriously disappeared without a trace.
The Silent
1-2 The Hunters come across a traveling circus. Gustav, the 
director, is desperate because the previous night someone 
kidnapped Lucina, an unusual young woman with the power 
to control insects, and someone he thinks of as a daughter. 
Lucina has no control over her ability and now Gustav fears it 
will cause genuine harm. 3-4 In a small village, a rumor has 
spread that a beautiful young girl often baths in the nearby 
river. Many bold young people went to see, but none came 
back. 5-6 Marina the Sea Explorer contacts the Hunters via 
a magical seagull. The bird reveals to the Hunters that, in the 
village they are heading to, a dangerous killer with whom she 
has unfinished business is hiding. As a reward for eliminating 
this threat, Marina will be willing to donate one of her prized 
treasures.

The Wise Fox
1-2 In a smallpox-stricken village, the Hunters receive a call 
for help from the parents of a dying child. A fox spirit who has 
often helped the village can be found in a nearby mountain 
spring, but no one has the strength for such a difficult journey. 
3-4 The Hunters find along their way a bag full of coins and, 
shortly after, a poor blind man who claims to have lost them. 
Doing the right or wrong thing will inform the Fox’s judgment 
of the Hunters. 5-6 The Hunters find a wounded, old fox 
being chased by a hunter named Galvan. The man will say 
that the creature is an evil spirit and that it must die. The Fox 
will take cover behind the Hunters.
Fire Eater the Puppeteer
1-2 Marilena, a desperate mother, runs through the town 
streets. Her two beloved twin children, Rosa and Rosario, 
disappeared from the bed they slept in without leaving a 
trace. And to think that, just the night before, the family had 
enjoyed the show of an itinerant puppeteer... 3-4 Wandering 
around a large market, the Hunters notice a disturbing 
puppet with a sad look that seems to be watching them. 
5-6 The Hunters stumble upon Fire Eater’s street show and 
realize that his amazing wireless puppets are animated by 
dark magic.
The Masked Swashbuckler
1-2 The Hunters witness the duel between the Masked 
Swashbuckler and a local noble, who is guilty of killing the 
goat of a poor peasant family for fun. 3-4 The Masked 
Swashbuckler, hired by an NPC from a past Scenario, 
tries to assassinate one of the Hunters. 5-6 The Masked 
Swashbuckler, drunk in the tavern, remembers the past and 
the betrayals he suffered. If the Hunters keep him company, 
between one slurred word and the next, the swordsman will 
let slip a Clue about the One Who Plots in the Shadows.
Orcus the Guardian
1-2 The Hunters learn that a fearsome monster is garrisoning 
a bridge across the river and killing anyone who doesn’t pay 
him a toll. In reality, Orcus has settled under the bridge to live 
far from the village and avoid doing harm. But when he went 
out to ask for alms from a passing merchant, the situation as 
always ended badly... and the rumor of a ferocious creature 
guarding the bridge spread. 3-4 The Hunters arrive in 
a square where an execution is about to take place. Orcus 
unintentionally killed the arrogant son of the local noble 
and was arrested. But the villagers plead for the good giant’s 
fate, who over the years has always done his utmost to help 
everyone. 5-6 The Hunters come across a criminal called 
Vizzini, who keeps the Masked Swashbuckler and the gigantic 
Orcus the Guardian in his service.
Goldilocks and her War Bears
1-2 The Hunters encounter a band of savagely slaughtered 
mercenaries. Jacques, their leader, will tell them of a young 
girl with three Bears who were able to hold their ground 
alone against thirty of his men. 3-4 The Hunters encounter 
Goldilocks while she is desperately seeking a cure for one of 
her Bears, who has fallen ill. 5-6 The Hunters are in a tavern 
when their waitress accidentally pours soup on a girl’s hair. 
But the waitress’ apologies are far from enough to appease 
Goldilocks’ ire.
Sinbad the Traveler of a Thousand Seas
1-2 In an old village tavern, Sinbad delights patrons with 
tales of his adventures in the Far East. All his specific and 
detailed references to magic and fantastic creatures lead 
the Hunters to understand that Sinbad isn’t merely telling 
tales. 3-4 The Hunters will encounter Sinbad in the palace 
of a local noble. The Traveler boasts of having brought the 
talented Chronicler Sherazade to Europe, and of knowing

to reveal this information, but only if someone can relieve

being is putting the whole region to fire and the sword. Sinbad

Event
An Event is a series of Scenes that the Storyteller can 
introduce to act as a “link” to the general plot of the 
Campaign. These Scenes are great for:
 ◆ Introducing opponents such as Sherazade and Saint 
George.
 ◆ Introducing the One Who Plots in the Shadows.
 ◆ Letting the Hunters meet the ghost of the Child Savior.
 ◆ Having the Hunters meet another Hunter or a 
Dark Presence with useful information for their 
investigation.
Some Events will be simple Scenes, such as meeting in an 
inn with a Hunter to exchange information. In this case, the 
Storyteller can take advantage of the setting to give color 
and depth to the world of Broken Europe, and to the stories 
of other Hunters or to Dark Presences.
Events with Sherazade or Saint George as protagonists, 
on the other hand, could generate meaningful Scenarios 
that must be resolved before the Hunters can resume their 
journey.
Tackling a Hunter-specific issue 
The Hunters encounter a situation related to one of their 
own. Roll a die or pick from the list:
Baba Yaga the Child Witch
1-2 A witch who claims to have killed the famous Baba 
Yaga. 3-4 A family that mistakes little Yaga for their dead 
daughter. 5-6 A lady who knows of Morgana’s secret and of 
Yaga and Merlin’s sins.
Babai the Judge
1-2 A corrupt judge who uses the law for personal gain. 
3-4 A feud between two villages that has not been settled 
for over a hundred years because no one is willing to act as a 
judge between the parties. 5-6 An elusive murderer named 
Mormo, who is said to walk in the shadows to escape the law.
Garou the Old Wolf
1-2 A young wolf from the North named Fenrir who wants to 
beat the famous Garou. 3-4 An old hunter who is after the 
trophy he most desires, the skin of the wolf that has terrified 
humans for decades. 5-6 A ruthless brigand who uses the big 
bad wolf as a cautionary tale to prey on the weak.
James the Swordsman
1-2 A crew of smugglers who want to avenge their captain, 
killed in a duel. 3-4 A monstrous beast that persecutes an 
innocent, just as it happened to James. 5-6 A famous duelist 
who, however, wins his encounters through deceit.
The Astonishing Pied Piper Without a Name

against man. 3-4 A young apprentice who has heard the

Bond with the Dark Presence. This Descriptor can be

C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
260
work behind the scenes to grant bonuses.
 ◆ The Storyteller can manage the Hunter backstage by 
creating ad hoc Scenes for them, or by making them 
return to “report” to the lead Hunter at the beginning 
of the following Scene. This way, the Hunter may 
have achieved all or part of the assigned task and the 
Storyteller, in keeping with the narrative, will be able to 
inflict Wounds, retrieve Soma or activate Gifts.
 Forge a Bond with another Hunter
Going through the investigation together created a 
special relationship between two Hunters. To take this 
Advancement, the Player of the Hunter affected by the 
Bond must agree.
Having a Bond with another Hunter grants the following 
benefits:
 ◆ Once per Scenario, a Hunter can come to the rescue of 
their Bond wherever they are, appearing in the Scene 
when needed most.
 ◆ Once per Scene, a Hunter can give 1 or more Soma to 
their Bond to help them out in a situation.
 ◆ Once per Scene, a Hunter can take 1 Wound in place 
of their Bond, but only if it makes sense in the story for 
them to be able to stand between them and the threat.
For a two-way Bond, each affected Hunter must purchase 
the Advancement.
 Forge a Bond with a Dark Presence
A Hunter can create a special relationship with a Dark 
Presence they have encountered on their travels. The 
conditions for creating the Bond must be established by the 
Player in agreement with the Storyteller. Once the Bond 
is created, the Dark Presence will become an ally of the 
Hunter, following them on their adventures.
 ◆ If the Dark Presence was an Opponent or Neutral, the 
Storyteller will have to consider whether to modify 
some of their Gifts to make them more suitable for 
their new role.
 ◆ The Hunter must create a Descriptor to represent their 
Bond with the Dark Presence. This Descriptor can be 
brought into play during actions and marked to obtain 
XP like all the others.
 ◆ The Dark Presence will do their best to support the 
Hunter, within the limits of what the Bond Descriptor 
states. However, the NPC remains under the 
Storyteller’s control.
Limitations when choosing Advancements
With the exception of Increase the Soma value by 1, each 
Advancement can only be chosen once. A Hunter can never 
have more than 4 Gifts (with the exception of those obtained 
temporarily during a Scenario), so upon obtaining the fifth 
Gift the Hunter must choose which one is being replaced 
from among those they already have. The Dark Ego Gift  
can never be replaced in any way, but its Descriptor can be 
evolved through a Specialization.

Escenario: una historia completa de 
Broken Tales que reinterpreta y “rom-
pe” un cuento de hadas clásico.
Éxitos adicionales:  éxitos que se 
añaden a los Éxitos básicos, obtenidos 
gastando Soma, tirando dados o gra-
cias al efecto de los dones.
Éxitos básicos: el número de Éxitos 
con los que empieza un Cazador cuan-
do se enfrenta a una prueba de posi-
ción o defensa.
Fracaso: indica que la acción de un 
Cazador no ha tenido éxito debido a 
una prueba de posición o defensa 
fallida.
Heridas: la aptitud de una criatura 
(Cazador o PNJ) para resistir ante las 
fuentes de adversidad, ya sean físicas o 
mentales.
Intercambio: una breve unidad de 
tiempo que define el orden de las ac-
ciones de cada participante en una si-
tuación de conflicto.
Interludio: un momento específico 
durante la sesión en el que los Cazadores 
toman aliento, gastan PX, recuperan 
Soma y se curan Heridas.
Jugador: cualquier participante en 
una sesión de Broken Tales. Uno de los 
jugadores debe tomar el papel de 
Narrador, a menudo el que ha leído 
este libro en su totalidad. Los demás 
participantes interpretan a un Cazador.
Nivel de Oposición: abreviado como 
NO, es el valor de dificultad asociado 
a un PNJ o amenaza.
Objetivo: descripción de los propósi-
tos y metas de un PNJ.
Palabras clave: palabras sueltas que 
ayudan a los jugadores a centrarse en 
los detalles narrativos de forma directa, 
estimulando la imaginación y ayudan-
do a todos los implicados a crear deta-
lles interesantes sobre la marcha.
Penalizador:  valor a restar de los 
Éxi tos básicos, derivado de los dones 
de los PNJs.
PNJ: el acrónimo de personaje no 
jugador. Todos los personajes no pro-
tagonistas, criaturas fantásticas, pre-
sencias oscuras y, en general, cualquier 
personaje interpretado por el Narrador. 
Los PNJs se dividen en tres tipos: prin-
cipales, menores y presencias oscuras.
Presencia oscura: PNJ relacionado 
con el mundo de los cuentos de hadas 
y renacido en su versión “rota” en el 
mundo de Broken Tales.
Prueba de defensa: la mecánica de 
juego mediante la cual un Cazador re-
acciona a las acciones de un PNJ o a 
una amenaza, que gestiona el Narrador.
Prueba de posición: la mecánica de 
juego por la que un Cazador intenta 
tener éxito en las acciones que ha des-
crito en la narración.
Puntos de Experiencia: abreviado 
como PX, es el valor numérico que in-
dica la experiencia obtenida por un 
Cazador durante sus aventuras.
Sesión: una tarde o noche de juego.
 Soma:  un valor que cuantifica la 
fuerza interior y la aptitud para apro-
vechar las energías internas de un 
Cazador. Gastar Soma permite obtener 
resultados positivos automáticos du-
rante las pruebas o activar algunos 
dones.
Triunfo: indica un buen resultado en 
una prueba de posición o defensa y 
puede ser de tres tipos: con un coste, 
estándar o con un incremento.
